questions.question: Which empire was ruled by Genghis Khan and became the largest contiguous land empire in history?
questions.explanation: The Mongol Empire, founded by Genghis Khan in 1206, stretched from the Pacific Ocean to Eastern Europe at its height — roughly 24 million square kilometers. It was held together by the Mongol postal relay system (yam), religious tolerance, and meritocracy. After Genghis died in 1227, his empire was divided among his sons but continued to expand.
questions.memory_hook: Mongol Empire = biggest land empire ever. Genghis Khan started with a collection of nomadic tribes and conquered most of the known world in one generation.

questions.question: The French Revolution began in which year?
questions.explanation: The French Revolution began in 1789 with the storming of the Bastille on July 14 — now celebrated as Bastille Day. It ended the absolute monarchy in France and established the principles of liberty, equality, and fraternity. The Revolution eventually gave rise to Napoleon Bonaparte, who declared himself Emperor in 1804.
questions.memory_hook: 1789 = French Revolution. Think: 1776 is American, 1789 is French. Three years after the American Revolution inspired a generation of French intellectuals.

questions.question: Which ancient wonder was located in Alexandria, Egypt?
questions.explanation: The Lighthouse of Alexandria (Pharos of Alexandria) stood approximately 100-140 meters tall on the island of Pharos in Alexandria's harbor. Built around 280 BC, it guided ships into the harbor for over 1,500 years. It was one of the Seven Wonders of the Ancient World and collapsed in a series of earthquakes between 956 and 1323 AD.
questions.memory_hook: Alexandria's wonder = the Lighthouse. The world's first great lighthouse, so famous that 'pharos' became the word for lighthouse in French, Spanish, and Italian.

questions.question: The Berlin Wall, which divided East and West Germany, fell in which year?
questions.explanation: The Berlin Wall fell on November 9, 1989, after the East German government announced that citizens could cross the border freely. Crowds of East and West Germans tore the wall down with pickaxes and hammers. The wall had stood since 1961, built by the communist East German government to prevent citizens from fleeing to the West.
questions.memory_hook: Berlin Wall fell in 1989. Built in 1961 to keep people in, torn down 28 years later by the people it imprisoned. One of the most watched events in television history.

questions.question: The FIFA World Cup is held every how many years?
questions.explanation: The FIFA World Cup takes place every four years, with the host country chosen years in advance by FIFA. The first World Cup was held in 1930 in Uruguay. The tournament features 32 national teams (expanded to 48 starting in 2026) competing over about a month. Brazil has won the most titles with five championships.
questions.memory_hook: World Cup = every 4 years. Like the Olympics. Only 32 teams make it (soon 48). Brazil wins it most — 5 times. Argentina and Germany each have 4.

questions.question: In tennis, what is the term for winning a game without the opponent scoring a single point?
questions.explanation: A 'love game' in tennis is a game won without the opponent scoring any points (0 points = love). The term 'love' for zero in tennis likely comes from the French 'l'oeuf' (the egg), referring to the egg-shaped zero. A set won 6-0 is called a 'bagel' and winning 6-0, 6-0 is called a 'double bagel.'
questions.memory_hook: Love game = opponent scores zero. 'Love' = zero in tennis, from the French word for egg. A bagel (6-0 set) is a whole egg. A double bagel (6-0, 6-0) is total domination.

questions.question: The 'Shot Heard Round the World' in baseball refers to whose legendary 1951 home run?
questions.explanation: Bobby Thomson hit 'The Shot Heard Round the World' on October 3, 1951, a walk-off three-run home run in the ninth inning that gave the New York Giants the National League pennant over the Brooklyn Dodgers. The radio call by Russ Hodges ('The Giants win the pennant!') became one of the most iconic moments in baseball history.
questions.memory_hook: Bobby Thomson, 1951. The Giants win the pennant! The most dramatic home run in baseball history. Thomson was relatively unknown — the moment made him immortal.

questions.question: Titanic (1997), directed by James Cameron, was the first film to gross over $1 billion worldwide. Who played Jack Dawson?
questions.explanation: Leonardo DiCaprio played Jack Dawson alongside Kate Winslet's Rose DeWitt Bukater in Titanic (1997). The film cost $200 million to make and grossed $2.2 billion worldwide, winning 11 Academy Awards including Best Picture and Best Director. It held the record as the highest-grossing film ever until Cameron's own Avatar surpassed it in 2010.
questions.memory_hook: DiCaprio = Jack in Titanic. 11 Oscars, $2.2 billion. Cameron bet everything on a movie about a ship that sank. It became the highest-grossing film in history at the time.

questions.question: Which rock band released 'Bohemian Rhapsody' in 1975?
questions.explanation: 'Bohemian Rhapsody' was written by Freddie Mercury and released by Queen in 1975. At nearly 6 minutes long with no chorus, it defied all pop conventions and became one of the best-selling singles of all time. It has been called the greatest rock song ever written. It charted again decades later after being featured in the 1992 film Wayne's World.
questions.memory_hook: 'Bohemian Rhapsody' = Queen, 1975. No chorus, opera section, hard rock, piano ballad — all in one song. Radio stations said it was too long to play. They were wrong.

questions.question: Which 1994 Quentin Tarantino film features a hitman duo, a Royale with Cheese, and a briefcase with a mysterious glow?
questions.explanation: Pulp Fiction (1994) is Quentin Tarantino's landmark film starring John Travolta, Samuel L. Jackson, Uma Thurman, and Bruce Willis. The non-linear narrative follows interconnected stories of crime and redemption. It revitalized Travolta's career, was nominated for 7 Academy Awards (winning Best Original Screenplay), and is frequently listed among the greatest films ever made.
questions.memory_hook: Pulp Fiction = Tarantino, 1994. Royale with Cheese = McDonald's in France. The briefcase glows but we never see what's inside. Tarantino's rule: the audience's imagination is better than any prop.

questions.question: Which mountain range separates Europe from Asia?
questions.explanation: The Ural Mountains in Russia form the traditional boundary between Europe and Asia, running approximately 2,500 km from the Arctic Ocean to the northern edge of the Kazakh steppe. They are relatively ancient and worn down — the highest peak, Mount Narodnaya, is only 1,895 meters. The Caucasus Mountains also serve as part of the boundary in the south.
questions.memory_hook: Ural Mountains = Europe meets Asia. The dividing line is in Russia. One side of the mountains = Europe, other side = Asia. The same country spans two continents.

questions.question: What is the name of the force that slows objects down when they rub against a surface?
questions.explanation: Friction is the force that resists the relative motion of surfaces in contact. It is caused by microscopic irregularities in surfaces catching on each other. Friction can be useful (it's what lets tires grip the road and lets you walk without sliding) or unwanted (it wastes energy as heat in engines). Friction doesn't exist in space in a vacuum.
questions.memory_hook: Friction = resistance between surfaces. Without it, you couldn't walk, tires couldn't grip, and you couldn't hold a pen. It's what makes the physical world usable — and also what makes engines inefficient.
